---
# Authored content. (Editors: publication fields live in _metadata.yml.)
# If you submit a .docx/.tex instead, this file is generated for you — delete it.
title: "Your article title"
author:
  - name: { given: First, family: Author }
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    email: first.author@example.org
    corresponding: true
    affiliations: [{ ref: aff1 }]
  - name: { given: Second, family: Author }
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0001
    affiliations: [{ ref: aff2 }]
affiliations:
  - { id: aff1, name: "First University" }
  - { id: aff2, name: "Second University" }
abstract: >
  One-paragraph abstract.
keywords: [keyword one, keyword two, keyword three]
bibliography: references.bib
---

Write the article body in Markdown. Cite with `[@key]` (parenthetical) or
`@key` (in-text), where keys come from `references.bib`.

## Introduction

Your text here [@example2024].

## Methods

## Results

## Discussion

## Declarations {.unnumbered}

### Author Contributions {.unnumbered}

- First Author: ...
- Second Author: ...

### Funding {.unnumbered}

### Potential conflicts of interest {.unnumbered}

### Declaration of AI use {.unnumbered}

## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
